stdout:
Your roadmap is too revenue-led before trust, measurement, and business-owner conversion are ready.

The current app is already a useful public directory: static pages, search, filters, map, contact routing, ratings, amenities, QR assets. But it does not yet prove traffic, scan attribution, owner claims, correction workflow, or sales conversion. That makes “premium listings first” premature unless the offer is extremely manual and founder-sold.

The biggest ordering problem: premium listings and QR affiliate network depend on analytics, claim flow, and owner confidence. Right now the QR redirect pages are just instant meta-refresh pages with a “future tracking hook.” Without scan/click counts, you cannot credibly sell “featured placement,” “QR performance,” or affiliate value. Build the measurement layer before monetization, or at least as the first slice of monetization.

Second problem: classifieds, creative layer, Reddit/Discord, and tarot are distractions from the directory’s core wedge. They may add community flavor, but they add moderation/content obligations before the main product has a business-owner loop. The app’s strongest current asset is not community; it is a high-coverage local business graph with contact channels.

Third problem: the roadmap undervalues boring operational features: claim/correction intake, data freshness, duplicate detection, status confidence, admin review, and sales collateral. These are less exciting than AI or affiliates, but they directly improve trust and make field sales possible.

Missing features I would add:

Owner claim and correction workflow. Even a static mailto/form endpoint is enough at first, but it should capture business slug, owner name, phone, requested edits, and proof. This is essential for accuracy and premium upsell.

Analytics and attribution. Track QR scans, business page views, outbound WhatsApp/call/Instagram/Booking clicks, source page, timestamp, device-ish info, and campaign/sticker ID. This is the foundation for premium listings, affiliate network, and sales conversations.

Featured placement system. Not just “premium listing” as a business idea, but actual ranking slots, sponsored badges, category/area boosts, and clear inventory rules so the product cannot be gamed or become messy.

Business owner dashboard or report card. Even if generated as static/private HTML or PDF: “Your listing got X views, Y WhatsApp clicks, Z QR scans, missing website, missing Instagram verification.” This converts data into perceived value.

Field-sales kit. Printable QR sheets, sticker labels, one-page owner pitch, Spanish/English WhatsApp templates, pricing sheet, and a tracking ID per sticker batch. This is not just design; it is revenue infrastructure.

Data QA/admin queue. Show missing/low-confidence records, stale verification dates, duplicate candidates, closed businesses, missing coordinates, bad phone formats, unverified Instagram handles. This makes the directory maintainable.

SEO basics. Sitemap, robots.txt, canonical URLs, structured data/LocalBusiness schema, Open Graph previews, category/area landing pages. You already have hundreds of static pages; SEO is one of the highest-ROI distribution channels.

Tourist intent pages. “Where to eat in Cocles,” “Hostels in Puerto Viejo,” “Tours near Punta Uva,” “Places with WhatsApp.” These are more useful than generic search for visitors and easier to rank/share.

Saved/shareable search URLs. Current filters appear client-side only. Make category/area/channel/search state URL-addressable so sales links, SEO-ish pages, and WhatsApp sharing work better.

Language layer. Spanish/English labels and owner-facing messages matter in Puerto Viejo. The app currently reads mostly English, while the business/operator side likely needs Spanish-first affordances.

Feasibility/ROI corrections:

Premium listings: ROI could be 5, but feasibility is understated. It is not “payments + analytics.” It also needs proof of value, sales workflow, fulfillment rules, support, and renewal handling. I would score feasibility 4 until analytics and claim flow exist.

QR affiliate network: feasibility is also understated. QR generation is easy; commission attribution and merchant trust are not. If the affiliate is just “tracked leads,” feasibility 2. If money changes hands, feasibility 4 and ops risk rises.

AI service upsells: overvalued for now. OpenAI integration is not the hard part. Packaging, onboarding, business-specific context, multilingual tone, and support are the hard parts. Good Phase 2, not before owner relationships exist.

Instagram capture engine: strategically valuable but correctly deferred. However, you may not need a full brittle scraper. A lighter “manual/semiautomated Instagram freshness field + owner-submitted events” may capture much of the value with lower risk.

Town API: undervalued as a standalone product, but overvalued if no partner is waiting. It is easy technically, but APIs do not create demand by themselves. Build only if it supports widgets, concierge, or internal app features.

Refreshable scanner: under-prioritized. The enrichment system is already partly built and resumable. Turning it into a reliable refresh/report workflow improves trust, powers “last verified” claims, and creates the playbook for other towns. I would move a narrow version earlier.

Classifieds/community/creative layer: feasibility is right, ROI is probably inflated unless you commit to moderation and seeding. Low code does not mean low attention cost.

Print magazine: maybe undervalued strategically but should follow proof. It makes sense after you know which categories/businesses engage and you have scan/click data to sell ads.

Recommended top 3 things to build right now:

1. Analytics and attribution layer.

Add tracked redirect endpoints/pages for QR scans and outbound clicks: WhatsApp, phone, Instagram, Booking, website, Maps. Since this is GitHub Pages/static, use a lightweight external-free option if possible, such as encoded static redirect URLs plus server logs if hosted elsewhere, or a no-paid endpoint you control later. At minimum, structure the app so every outbound action routes through a trackable URL with business slug, channel, and campaign ID.

This unlocks premium listings, QR stickers, affiliate experiments, owner reports, and prioritization.

2. Claim/correction plus owner report flow.

Replace the generic mailto with a real structured claim/correction path. Generate an owner-facing report for every business: current contact channels, missing fields, QR download, page link, and eventually performance metrics. This makes the product legible to businesses and gives you a natural sales conversation: “Here is your page; claim it; fix it; upgrade it.”

This is higher ROI than building payments immediately.

3. SEO/intent landing pages with shareable filters.

Create static category/area pages and URL-addressable filters: restaurants in Puerto Viejo, hotels in Cocles, tours in Punta Uva, businesses with WhatsApp, etc. Add sitemap and LocalBusiness schema on business pages. You already have the dataset and static generation model, so this is a very feasible way to turn the directory into discoverable utility instead of only a link someone already has.

My revised order would be: instrumentation, claim/report flow, SEO/intent pages, then premium listings. QR affiliate comes after you have scan/outbound data. AI, concierge, and second-town scanner come after the Puerto Viejo owner loop proves repeatable.
